Orchestrator designs the team dynamically per problem; CRITIC is the only invariant role. Delivers via ExitPlanMode; never crosses into implementation.

Registers all eight skills under the waza namespace, callable as /waza:think, /waza:check, /waza:hunt, /waza:design, /waza:read, /waza:write, /waza:learn, and /waza:health. For one skill on Claude Code v2.1.143 or newer, use /plugin install waza-<name>@waza.

plugins.name: waza-check
plugins.description: Reviews code diffs, PRs, issue queues, release readiness, commits, pushes, publishing, and project audits. Use when users ask review/看看代码/合并前/看看issue/PR/release/push or to implement an approved plan, with safety gates for dirty and untracked worktrees. Not for exploring ideas, debugging root causes, or prose review.

plugins.name: waza-design
plugins.description: Produces distinctive, production-grade UI for pages, components, visual interfaces, typography, and screenshot-driven polish. Use when users ask 设计/做页面/做组件/UI/前端/截图 or say a screen is ugly, unclear, inconsistent, or visually wrong. Not for backend logic or data pipelines.

plugins.name: waza-health
plugins.description: Runs a budget-aware agent-assisted engineering health audit for instruction/config drift, hooks/MCP, verifier surfaces, and AI maintainability. Use when users ask 检查claude/检查codex/检查pi/配置检查/健康度 or report agents ignoring instructions, missing validation, or code becoming hard to maintain. Not for debugging code or reviewing PRs.

plugins.name: waza-hunt
plugins.description: Finds root cause before applying fixes for errors, crashes, regressions, failing tests, broken behavior, and screenshot-reported defects. Use when users ask 排查/报错/崩溃/不工作/回归/判断为什么报错, or say something used to work and now fails. Not for code review or new features.

plugins.name: waza-learn
plugins.description: Runs a six-phase research workflow that turns unfamiliar domains, source bundles, or collected material into publish-ready output. Use when users ask 学习一下/深入研究/研究一下/整理成文章/deep dive/compile sources or need one coherent reference from many inputs. Not for quick lookups or single-file reads.

plugins.name: waza-read
plugins.description: Reads URLs and PDFs by fetching source content, defaulting to concise summaries for plain read requests and clean Markdown when asked to convert, save, quote, cite, or feed downstream work. Use when users ask 看这个链接/读一下/read this/check this URL. Not for local text files already in the repo.

plugins.name: waza-think
plugins.description: Turns rough ideas into approved, decision-complete plans with validated structure before coding. Use when users ask 出方案/给方案/深入分析/怎么设计/有没有必要/值不值得/plan this/how should I/should we keep this for features, architecture, or value judgments. Not for bug fixes or small edits.

plugins.name: waza-write
plugins.description: Rewrites and polishes prose in Chinese or English, removing AI-like wording while preserving intent for drafts, docs, release notes, launch copy, and social posts. Use when users ask 帮我写/改稿/润色/去AI味/写一段/审稿/tweet/rewrite/proofread. Not for code comments, commit messages, or inline docs.

plugins.name: pm-skills
plugins.description: 63 PM skills (30 phase + 8 foundation + 10 utility + 15 tool) plus 4 sub-agents (pm-critic, pm-skill-auditor, pm-changelog-curator, pm-release-conductor) covering the full product lifecycle. v2.22.0 removes the 63 redundant per-skill command wrappers (each skill now appears once, invoked by name: /pm-skills:<name> on Claude Code, $<name> on Codex) and adds a native Codex .codex-plugin manifest. v2.21.0 is an additive distribution launch: pm-skills becomes installable through the new product-on-purpose marketplace while this self-hosted path keeps working unchanged; no new skills (catalog stays 63). v2.20.0 adds slash commands for the three workshop sprint methodologies plus documentation-count validator hardening; no new skills (catalog stays 63). v2.19.0 hardened release-validation tooling so the library polices its own counts, cross-references, and links. v2.18.0 added four highest-consensus content skills (discover-market-sizing, define-prioritization-framework, discover-journey-map, measure-survey-analysis), each with three thread-aligned samples. It carries forward v2.17.0 native Claude Code sub-agent registration: sub-agent definitions moved to the canonical agents/ directory (the coordination directory was renamed to _agent-context/ to free the name), so all 4 sub-agents auto-discover via @-mention on Claude Code; dispatch skills continue to provide the same capability on Codex CLI, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, and Gemini CLI. v2.17.0 also migrates skill frontmatter to the metadata-nested structure per the agentskills.io specification and makes the CI validators bash-3.2 portable. Doc-stack runs on Astro 6.3.x + Starlight 0.39.x. Carries forward v2.15.0 Sprint Skills (Foundation Sprint family of 7 + Design Sprint family of 7 + tool-note-and-vote standalone implementing Knapp/Zeratsky/Kowitz canonical sprint methodologies under classification:tool taxonomy), v2.12.0 OKR Skills set, v2.11.0 Meeting Skills Family, lean canvas, persona, 12 workflows including the foundation-to-design end-to-end arc, thread-aligned samples (Brainshelf + Storevine + Workbench), and lifecycle tools for creating, validating, and iterating skills. Follows the agentskills.io specification.

hooks.name: lint-check
hooks.description: Run linting checks after code edits to catch issues early.
hooks.content: Execute ESLint, Pylint, or other language-specific linters after file modifications to enforce code style rules and catch potential bugs.

hooks.name: log-commands
hooks.description: Log all executed commands and tool invocations to an audit file.
hooks.content: Record all bash commands executed during a Claude Code session to a timestamped audit log for debugging and compliance purposes.

hooks.name: test-runner
hooks.description: Automatically run relevant tests after code changes.
hooks.content: Detect changed files and run the associated test suite automatically after code modifications to catch regressions immediately.
